source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure installation of the AWS Replication Agent

input_row.source: CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure installation of the AWS Replication Agent
input_row.description: Set up and verify the installation of the AWS Replication Agent on all relevant systems to facilitate efficient and reliable data replication. This process includes downloading the agent, configuring it according to best practices, and ensuring it is correctly integrated with your AWS environment. Regularly check the agent’s performance and update it as needed to maintain optimal functionality and data integrity during replication processes.
input_row.rationale: Installing the AWS Replication Agent is crucial for enabling efficient and reliable data replication, ensuring that critical data is accurately duplicated across systems. Proper configuration and integration with your AWS environment optimize the agent’s performance, enhancing data availability and disaster recovery capabilities. Regular checks and updates of the replication agent help maintain its effectiveness, ensuring data integrity and minimizing the risk of replication failures.
input_row.audit: 1. On the source servers page, from Actions, choose add servers to obtain the agent installer link. 2. On your source server (in our case, the EC2 instance that was already created) download the appropriate agent installer for your operating system. o For Linux instance on US-East-1. Substitute your region in the {Region} brackets of this command: wget -O ./aws-replication-installer-init https://aws-elastic-disaster- recovery-us-east-1.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/linux/aws-replication- installer-init  3. Run following command: chmod +x aws-replication-installer-init; sudo ./aws-replication-installer- init  4. Type in your region. Region is case sensitive: if you’re in us-east-1, make sure you type “us-east-1”. 5. If you’re using SSH, you will be prompted with your activation ID and secret activation key. Make sure you have those accessible for the IAM user you’re using. You can generate a new key from the IAM dashboard if you forgot to save your key. 6. Select “Enter” to replicate all servers. 7. All servers should replicate. 8. Make sure your OS is up to date. If you run into an error replicating your devices, view the documentation on troubleshooting the AWS replication installation here: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/mgn/latest/ug/installation-requirements.html. 9. If install runs successfully, the source server will appear in your Elastic Disaster Recovery Console dashboard on the “source servers” page. This will signify the beginning of the replication process.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control requires setting up and verifying the installation of the AWS Replication Agent on relevant systems. This involves downloading and executing an installer script on source servers, a task typically performed via SSH access to the server instances. The process outlined requires interaction with the system's operating system, installation and execution of the installer script, verification of replication processes, and ensuring that the environment (including the OS) is up-to-date. These tasks are not directly queryable via AWS APIs but instead require manual intervention and checks on the operating system level.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **Access the Source Server:**
   - Use SSH to connect to the EC2 instance (source server) where the AWS Replication Agent should be installed.

2. **Download and Install AWS Replication Agent:**
   - Obtain the installer link for your region from the AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery console.
   - Execute the download command provided for Linux (e.g., `wget -O ./aws-replication-installer-init https://aws-elastic-disaster-recovery-us-east-1.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/linux/aws-replication-installer-init`).
   - Run the installation commands: `chmod +x aws-replication-installer-init; sudo ./aws-replication-installer-init`.

3. **Configure and Verify Installation:**
   - Follow on-screen instructions during installation to specify the appropriate AWS region and provide your activation ID and secret activation key.
   - Verify that the source server appears in the Elastic Disaster Recovery Console dashboard under the “source servers” page, indicating successful agent installation and the beginning of the replication process.

4. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Ensure the server is up-to-date with the latest updates and patches.
   - Check replication statuses regularly and confirm they match expected performance criteria as described in documentation.

Since the installation and configuration verify actions on individual servers' operating systems and involve human decision-making, this control is most optimally audited manually.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as manual is partially correct. While the installation of the AWS Replication Agent requires manual intervention on the source server, certain aspects of the verification process can be automated using AWS APIs. Specifically, checking whether the source server appears in the Elastic Disaster Recovery Console can be automated using the AWS SDK (boto3) or AWS CLI to query the status of source servers.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI Capabilities:** The AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery service provides APIs that can be used to list and describe source servers, which can be leveraged to automate part of the compliance check. For example, using the `describe_source_servers` method in boto3 or the equivalent AWS CLI command can verify the presence and status of the replication agent.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- **Automation Details:** The manual steps are technically correct for the installation process, but the assessment lacks mention of automation possibilities for verification steps using AWS APIs.
- **Boto3 Methods or CLI Commands:** The assessment does not mention specific boto3 methods or CLI commands that could be used for verification. The `describe_source_servers` method in boto3 or `aws drs describe-source-servers` in the CLI could be used.
- **Manual Steps Accuracy:** The manual steps for installation are accurate and complete. However, the assessment could benefit from including verification steps using AWS APIs.
- **Program Name Convention:** The program name is not explicitly mentioned in the assessment. A more descriptive name could be `aws_replication_agent_installation_verification`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation of Verification:** Include steps to automate the verification of the replication agent installation using AWS APIs. This can reduce manual effort and improve accuracy.
- **Missing Considerations:** Consider using AWS Systems Manager for automating the installation process across multiple servers, which can further reduce manual intervention.
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:** Use the `describe_source_servers` method in boto3 or the AWS CLI to automate the verification of the replication agent's presence and status.
- **Better Program Name Suggestion:** `aws_replication_agent_installation_verification` to clearly indicate the focus on verifying the installation of the replication agent.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- While the manual steps are accurate, the lack of automation consideration for verification reduces confidence in the completeness of the assessment.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify:** Include automation steps for verifying the installation using AWS APIs. Specifically, use boto3 or AWS CLI to check the status of source servers in the Elastic Disaster Recovery service. This will enhance the audit process by combining manual installation with automated verification, ensuring a more comprehensive compliance check.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Manual

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
While the presence and status of source servers can be queried via AWS APIs (e.g., using boto3's describe_source_servers for Elastic Disaster Recovery), the actual installation of the AWS Replication Agent on the OS level (e.g., via SSH into EC2, downloading, and running the installer) cannot be automated or verified solely through AWS APIs. The agent's installation process requires direct interaction with the server's operating system, which is outside the scope of AWS API visibility. Therefore, full compliance with this control requires manual steps.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the Source Server:**  
   - SSH into each relevant EC2 instance (or other source server) where the AWS Replication Agent must be installed.

2. **Download and Install the AWS Replication Agent:**  
   - In the AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery Console, navigate to "Source servers" and select "Add servers" to obtain the correct installer link for your region.
   - On the server, run the provided wget command to download the installer (e.g.,  
     `wget -O ./aws-replication-installer-init https://aws-elastic-disaster-recovery-<region>.s3.<region>.amazonaws.com/latest/linux/aws-replication-installer-init`).
   - Make the installer executable (`chmod +x aws-replication-installer-init`) and run it (`sudo ./aws-replication-installer-init`).

3. **Configure and Complete Installation:**  
   - Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the installation, specifying the AWS region and any required activation information.

4. **Verify Installation:**  
   - Confirm that the agent is running on the server (e.g., check running processes or systemd services).
   - Optionally, verify in the AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery Console that the server appears as a registered source server.

5. **Document Compliance:**  
   - Record the date, server name, and installer version for audit purposes.

**WHY NOT AUTOMATED:**  
The installation and initial configuration of the AWS Replication Agent require direct OS-level access and execution, which cannot be performed or fully verified via AWS APIs alone. AWS APIs can confirm if a server is registered, but cannot guarantee the agent was installed according to best practices or that it is running correctly at the OS level.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
The first AI opinion correctly identified the manual nature of the installation process. The second AI noted that some verification can be automated via AWS APIs, but this does not cover the full scope of the control, which includes installation and configuration steps that are not API-accessible. Therefore, the final decision is manual, as full compliance cannot be achieved through automation alone.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS STORAGE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
